name: Deba Priyo Guha
summary: I am an observational cosmologist and astrophysicist-in-training working at the intersection of supernova cosmology, time-domain astronomy, and machine learning. My research centers on Type Ia supernovae as standardizable candles — the same tools used in the 2011 Nobel Prize-winning discovery of the accelerating expansion of the Universe — with a focus on understanding how host galaxy stellar populations bias their luminosity and affect precision cosmological measurements.

At CASSA (IUB), under Dr. Syed Ashraf Uddin, I investigate how host galaxy age correlates with SN Ia Hubble residuals using spectral energy distribution fitting on the CSP and SDSS photometric samples, directly addressing the 5σ Hubble tension between early- and late-universe probes of H₀. I build end-to-end Bayesian inference pipelines (Nautilus, MultiNest) and work with real survey photometry to constrain dark energy and test extensions of ΛCDM.

I also work on radio galaxy morphology classification (RGC, CASSA) and lunar hyperspectral mineralogy from Chandrayaan-1 M³ data (LunaXNet), applying deep learning where traditional methods fail. My published work (PIE-ADA, IEEE QPAIN 2026) demonstrates physics-informed ensemble learning for photometric transient classification across 14 astrophysical classes using the PLAsTiCC dataset.

Selected for the Bangladesh National Team for the International Olympiad on Astronomy & Astrophysics (IOAA 2017). I aim to pursue a PhD in observational cosmology or time-domain astrophysics, contributing to next-generation surveys (LSST/Rubin, Roman) that will transform our understanding of dark energy and the fate of the Universe.

specific: SN Ia luminosity standardization and host galaxy stellar population bias
Hubble constant tension (H₀ discrepancy) and ΛCDM extensions
Bayesian SED fitting and stellar population synthesis (BAGPIPES, CIGALE)
Photometric classification of transients for LSST/Rubin alert streams
Radio AGN morphology: FR I/II, WAT, NAT classification with deep learning
Cosmic distance ladder: Cepheids, TRGB, and type Ia calibration
Lunar and planetary hyperspectral mineralogy (Chandrayaan-1 M³)
Physics-informed and interpretable AI for scientific discovery
Large-scale structure and galaxy evolution across cosmic time
